stories.headline: Tesla Launches Fully Driverless Robotaxi in Miami, Skipping the Safety-Monitor Phase
stories.key_takeaway: Tesla launched fully driverless robotaxi rides in Miami on July 3, skipping the safety-monitor phase for the first time in a new city, betting its cheaper camera-only system can scale faster than lidar-equipped rivals despite an open NHTSA probe into poor-visibility performance.

pull_quote.text: Try it out if you are in Miami.
pull_quote.role: Vice President of AI Software, Tesla

data_points.label: Miami geofenced service area
After Austin, Houston, Dallas and Phoenix
Tesla stock rise after launch
Next Tesla earnings report

stories.article_excerpt: Tesla flipped a switch in Florida it had never flipped before, offering unsupervised robotaxi rides in Miami from day one.

stories.headline: LeapXpert Raises $180M to Bring AI Compliance Monitoring to Enterprise Messaging
stories.key_takeaway: LeapXpert raised $180M led by Riverwood Capital to evolve from off-channel messaging compliance into 'Governed Communication Intelligence,' using AI to mine governed WhatsApp, iMessage, Signal and WeChat conversations as an enterprise data asset.

pull_quote.text: AI agents are going to live right inside those channels, working alongside people in the conversations that matter most. That only works if every one of those conversations is governed and owned from the moment it starts. You can't bolt that on afterward, governance has to be there from the first message.

data_points.label: Growth round led by Riverwood Capital
Year LeapXpert was founded
Organizations on the platform, incl. banks and agencies
Prior 2023 raise, dwarfed by new round

stories.article_excerpt: LeapXpert closed a $180M round to turn governed WhatsApp and iMessage chats into AI-powered enterprise intelligence.

stories.headline: 8090 Solutions Lands $135M Led by Salesforce to Build Enterprise Software With AI Agent Swarms
stories.key_takeaway: 8090 raised a $135M Series A led by Salesforce to scale its Software Factory, where human engineers and swarms of AI agents build custom enterprise software on demand -- a direct bet that companies will build bespoke systems rather than buy packaged SaaS, with founder Chamath Palihapitiya taking the CEO role.

pull_quote.text: AI can write code. The hard part of enterprise software is keeping fifty agents and a hundred engineers changing the same complex system every week without it pulling apart.

data_points.label: Series A led by Salesforce
COBOL/Assembly reverse-engineered into 300k+ plain-English rules
Health insurer savings over four years
Founded; Palihapitiya became full-time CEO June 2026

stories.article_excerpt: The company Salesforce just backed with $135M wants to make packaged enterprise software optional.

stories.headline: Omen AI Raises $31M to Watch Data Center Coolant and Predict Hardware Failures
stories.key_takeaway: Omen AI raised a $31M Series A led by Nava Ventures to embed inline spectrometers in AI data center liquid-cooling loops, turning coolant chemistry into a real-time predictive-maintenance signal that catches bacterial growth and component wear before costly rack downtime.

pull_quote.text: You're not risking huge amounts of downtime because you have no insight into what's going on chemically.

data_points.label: Series A led by Nava Ventures
Total raised since 2024 founding
Data center assets monitored, 10-14 GW capacity
Elemental signatures tracked in real time

stories.article_excerpt: The most valuable machines of the AI era are kept alive by water, and Omen AI raised $31M to watch it.

stories.headline: ServiceNow and Accenture Launch a Forward Deployed Engineering Program to Scale Agentic AI
stories.key_takeaway: ServiceNow and Accenture are embedding their own engineers inside customers to build agentic AI in production, formalizing the 'forward deployed engineering' model that has become the dominant enterprise-AI go-to-market strategy in 2026 as vendors race to close the gap between AI pilots and real business impact.

pull_quote.text: The question our clients ask is not whether to invest in AI, it's how to make it work at enterprise scale. This program brings together Accenture's industry depth and implementation reach with ServiceNow's AI Platform to deliver real results, not roadmaps.
pull_quote.role: Lead for Software and Platform Engineering, Accenture

data_points.label: Leaders reporting sustained enterprise-wide AI impact
Pre-built AI agent skills on the ServiceNow AI Platform
Microsoft's rival Frontier Company FDE commitment
GenAI pilots with zero measurable P&L impact (MIT)

stories.article_excerpt: ServiceNow and Accenture didn't unveil a new model or chip -- they unveiled a staffing model for agentic AI.

stories.headline: Google Rolls Out Gemini 3.5 Pro With a 2M-Token Window at the Cheapest Frontier-Tier Price Yet
stories.key_takeaway: Gemini 3.5 Pro is nearing its delayed July 2026 launch with a confirmed 2M-token context window and Deep Think mode, but Google has not published benchmarks or pricing; whether it lands at a market-leading price will determine if it can claim the cheapest frontier-tier mantle amid an intensifying token-price war.

data_points.label: Context window, largest of any production frontier model
Conservative expected pricing per 1M tokens (unconfirmed)
Alphabet market value swing amid delay, June 22
Announced at Google I/O; GA slipped to July 2026

stories.article_excerpt: Google's delayed flagship nears launch with a 2M-token window and a pricing question at the heart of the frontier war.

stories.headline: Profound Launches 'Aim,' an Always-On Agent That Optimizes Brands for AI Search
stories.key_takeaway: Profound launched Aim, an always-on agent that turns AI-search signals into prioritized, executable marketing work, moving the well-funded GEO leader from measurement into agentic execution as ChatGPT, Gemini and Perplexity reshape SEO.

pull_quote.text: Marketing teams don't need another dashboard. They need to know what to do next. Aim works in the background, constantly looking for the moments that matter.

data_points.label: Series funding at $1B valuation (Feb 2026)
Total raised across four rounds
Enterprise customers, ~10% of Fortune 500
Profound Aim launch date

stories.article_excerpt: Profound launched Aim, an always-on agent that turns AI-search signals into prioritized marketing work for the ChatGPT era.

stories.headline: Candidly Expands Its AI 'Intelligence Center' With Six New Agents for Financial Guidance
stories.key_takeaway: Candidly added six new AI agents to its Intelligence Center on July 2, 2026, extending its student-debt roots into retirement, budgeting, benefits, and Trump Accounts, distributed through employers and institutions that reach 1 in 2 U.S. workers.

pull_quote.text: These six capabilities unlock what we've always believed is possible: enabling the industry to meet every participant and investor where they are, with holistic, N=1 personalized guidance that harmonizes across both sides of the balance sheet.

data_points.label: New AI agent capabilities added
U.S. workers Candidly is positioned to serve
Projected student-debt impact (2025)
AI chat users who move across financial topics

stories.article_excerpt: Candidly added six AI agents to its Intelligence Center, spanning retirement, budgeting, benefits, and Trump Accounts.

stories.headline: Boston Dynamics Puts the Electric Atlas Into Production With Google DeepMind's Gemini Brain
stories.key_takeaway: Boston Dynamics has moved its all-electric Atlas from research prototype to production humanoid, and partnered with Google DeepMind to run it on Gemini Robotics foundation models -- signaling that the 2026 humanoid race now hinges on whose AI 'brain' generalizes best, not whose hardware moves best.

pull_quote.text: We are building the world's most capable humanoid, and we knew we needed a partner that could help us establish new kinds of visual-language-action models for these complex robots. Nobody in the world is better suited than DeepMind.
pull_quote.role: Director of Robot Behavior for Atlas, Boston Dynamics

data_points.label: Degrees of freedom in the production Atlas
Maximum lift capacity of Atlas
Target year for Hyundai parts-sequencing deployment
Countries where Boston Dynamics' Spot already operates

stories.article_excerpt: Boston Dynamics rolled its production electric Atlas onstage -- and handed its brain to Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics.

stories.headline: ResearcherBench Puts 'Deep Research' AI Agents to the Test on Real Scientific Frontiers
stories.key_takeaway: ResearcherBench, the first benchmark testing 'deep research' AI agents on 65 frontier AI science questions, finds OpenAI and Gemini lead on insight quality but that even the best systems fail to ground most claims in verifiable citations.

pull_quote.text: Results show that OpenAI Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research significantly outperform other systems, with particular strength in open-ended consulting questions.

data_points.label: Expert-curated questions across 35 AI subjects
Top rubric coverage score (OpenAI Deep Research)
OpenAI Deep Research groundedness — ~2/3 of claims uncited
Question types: technical, literature review, open consulting

stories.article_excerpt: When OpenAI, Google and rivals shipped 'deep research' agents, nobody had a rigorous way to test whether they do science.

stories.headline: NVIDIA's Isaac for Healthcare Brings Multi-Agent AI Into the Operating Room
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA's Rheo blueprint within Isaac for Healthcare lets developers train multi-agent robots in photorealistic hospital digital twins before deploying them into the OR, with partners like PeritasAI, Lightwheel and AdventHealth pushing simulation-trained physical AI toward safety-critical surgery.

pull_quote.text: Instead of teaching robots inside hospitals, developers can now train hospitals -- in simulation -- before automation ever arrives.

data_points.label: Projected global clinician shortfall by 2030 (NVIDIA)
Trocar-assembly success gain from curriculum RL
OR productivity gain reported by partner Proximie
Estimated 2026 surgical-robotics market size

stories.article_excerpt: NVIDIA's Rheo blueprint trains hospital robots in digital twins before they reach the OR -- raising sim-to-real stakes.

stories.headline: COMPASS Tackles the Hardest Problem in AI Agents: Staying Coherent Over Long Horizons
stories.key_takeaway: COMPASS identifies context management, not model size, as the core bottleneck for long-horizon AI agents, and splits execution, oversight, and memory curation across three roles to report up to 20% accuracy gains over single- and multi-agent baselines.

pull_quote.text: We identify context management as the central bottleneck -- extended histories cause agents to overlook critical evidence or become distracted by irrelevant information, thus failing to replan or reflect from previous mistakes.

data_points.label: Reported accuracy gain over baselines
Benchmarks: GAIA, BrowseComp, Humanity's Last Exam
Components: Main Agent, Meta-Thinker, Context Manager
arXiv submission; under review for ACL

stories.article_excerpt: A research team names context management, not model size, as the real bottleneck for long-horizon AI agents.

stories.headline: UN Opens Its First Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva, With 169 Nations at the Table
stories.key_takeaway: The UN convened its first General Assembly-mandated multilateral forum on AI governance, drawing delegates from about 169 countries and pairing the talks with an independent 40-expert scientific panel's first report — but the dialogue is non-binding and produced no declaration, leaving open whether it can matter against the EU's binding AI Act and US deregulation.

pull_quote.text: When countries align on how to test systems, measure risk and assign responsibility, safety travels with the technology. When they do not, a patchwork of incompatible rules raises costs, divides the world — and protects no one.

data_points.label: Countries represented at the inaugural dialogue
Experts on the UN Independent Scientific Panel on AI
Of top-500 AI supercomputing power held by US and China
Second session, to be held in New York

stories.article_excerpt: For the first time, the world's governments sat under one roof to argue about how to govern artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: OpenAI Delays GPT-5.6's Public Launch After Washington Asks for Early Access and Oversight
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI limited GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) to about 20 government-vetted partners at Washington's request under a June 2026 executive order, marking the first live test of a voluntary 30-day pre-release federal review of a frontier commercial model.

pull_quote.text: We don't believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default. It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.

data_points.label: Government-vetted partners with GPT-5.6 access
Pre-release government review window (cut from 90)
GPT-5.6 Sol price per 1M input/output tokens
Executive Order signed (2026)

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI held GPT-5.6 back from public release, giving the US government first access under a new frontier-model framework.

stories.headline: A Federal AI Litigation Task Force Is Now Suing to Strike Down State AI Laws
stories.key_takeaway: A December 2025 executive order created a DOJ AI Litigation Task Force to sue states over their AI laws on constitutional grounds, and in April 2026 the DOJ made its first move by intervening against Colorado's algorithmic-discrimination statute, escalating a federal-vs-state fight over who regulates AI.

pull_quote.text: Laws that require AI companies to infect their products with woke DEI ideology are illegal. The Justice Department will not stand on the sidelines while states such as Colorado coerce our nation's technological innovators into producing harmful products.
pull_quote.role: Assistant Attorney General, DOJ Civil Rights Division

data_points.label: Executive order creating the AI Litigation Task Force
AI-related state laws enacted in 2025
Introduced 1,500+ AI bills by early 2026
DOJ intervenes in xAI v. Colorado (2026)

stories.article_excerpt: The most consequential fight in American tech policy is over who gets to write the rules for AI.
